title: DutyCycleTracker next_tx_available returns record start instead of end, allowing early TX
description: In rust/lichen-core/src/duty_cycle.rs:185, the code returns record.timestamp_ms + WINDOW_MS as when TX becomes available. But a record with duration D occupies the window until timestamp+D+WINDOW_MS. For a 200ms TX at t=0, current code says TX allowed at t=WINDOW_MS, but should be t=WINDOW_MS+200. This allows exceeding duty cycle by record.duration_ms worth of overlap. The eviction logic (line 211-212) correctly uses tx_end, but next_tx_available_ms doesn't match.
close_reason: Fixed in worktrees, verified by codereview passes
